# Skill manifest lint: per-file payload cap + section-trigger declarations (Sub-A of #11319)

## Context

Sub-A of Epic #11319 (Trigger-Aware Workflows graduation from Discussion #11314). Implements the **lint/schema primitive** — the mechanical-enforcement substrate that future migration sub-tickets (Sub-B onwards) consume.

**Strictly bounded scope:** schema extension + lint logic + `/create-skill` discipline update. NO workflow migrations in this ticket. Migration is Sub-B+ lane.

Per Discussion #11314 §8 graduation target + @neo-gpt body-repair #4: lint/schema primitive PR ≠ workflow-migration PR. Migration blast-radius bounded one-skill-per-PR.

## The Fix

### Schema extension (`skills.manifest.schema.json` + `skills.manifest.json`)

Add per-skill field:
- **`perFilePayloadBudget`**: positive integer (bytes); per-individual-file byte ceiling within `references/`. Calibrated empirical-floor per skill + 10-15% headroom. Optional with default `null` (no per-file enforcement) for v1 backwards-compat.

Add section-trigger declaration parser support. Canonical syntax per Discussion #11314 OQ1:
```markdown
## §<N> <Section Title>
<!-- trigger: [condition] → read [sub-rule.md] -->
```

### Lint logic extension (`ai/scripts/lint-skill-manifest.mjs`)

- **Per-file payload byte check**: walk `references/<file>.md`; if `wc -c` exceeds `perFilePayloadBudget` (when set) → fail with extraction recommendation
- **Section-trigger parse**: regex `/^<!-- trigger: (.+?) → read (.+?\.md) -->$/m` against post-header lines; build per-section index `{anchor, trigger, sub_rule_path, body_size_bytes}`
- **Trigger-frequency × size heuristic**: section with `body_size_bytes > 5_000` AND declared `trigger` matches rare-trigger pattern (heuristic: trigger string contains "openapi", "audit", "edge-case", "deprecation", etc. — extensible via manifest config) → flag as extraction candidate with Required-Action template
- **Required-Action template**:
  > *"`<skill>/references/<file>.md` §<N> is `<size>` bytes with declared trigger `<trigger>` (rare-firing class). Extract to sub-rule sibling file behind one-line trigger pointer per `skill-authoring-guide.md §Map vs World Atlas`. Reduce workflow body to: section header + `<!-- trigger: ... -->` pointer line."*

### `/create-skill` discipline update (`skill-authoring-guide.md`)

Append new subsection after existing §"Map vs World Atlas Constraint Placement":
- Recursive application rule (workflow files apply Map vs Atlas at section level)
- Canonical section-trigger syntax + example
- Cross-reference to lint enforcement: `lint-skill-manifest.mjs --base origin/dev` will flag rare-trigger sections exceeding threshold

### `.github/workflows/skill-manifest-lint.yml`

No structural change needed — workflow already invokes lint on `.agents/skills/**` PR changes. Extended lint behavior auto-fires.

## Contract Ledger Matrix (per Epic #11319 inheritance)

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Behavior | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `.agents/skills/skills.manifest.schema.json` | This sub-ticket | Add `perFilePayloadBudget` field schema; add section-trigger declaration support | Schema documented inline |
| `.agents/skills/skills.manifest.json` | This sub-ticket | Populate `perFilePayloadBudget` per-skill (calibrated empirical floor + 10-15% headroom; null where N/A for v1) | Per-skill calibration table in PR body |
| `ai/scripts/lint-skill-manifest.mjs` | This sub-ticket | Extend with per-file byte check + section-trigger parser + frequency × size heuristic | Lint output validation in PR diff |
| `.agents/skills/create-skill/references/skill-authoring-guide.md` | This sub-ticket | Append recursive-application subsection + canonical syntax + lint cross-ref | `/create-skill` discipline cross-link |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1: Schema field added**. `skills.manifest.schema.json` includes `perFilePayloadBudget` per-skill entry (positive integer; nullable for v1 opt-in)
- [ ] **AC2: Manifest populated**. `skills.manifest.json` per-skill entries set `perFilePayloadBudget` calibrated empirically (PR body documents the calibration table)
- [ ] **AC3: Lint walks `references/<file>.md` per skill**. Per-file byte check fires when `perFilePayloadBudget` is set
- [ ] **AC4: Section-trigger parser implemented**. Regex against `<!-- trigger: ... -->` HTML comments; parser indexes section anchor + trigger string + sub-rule path + body size
- [ ] **AC5: Trigger-frequency × size heuristic**. Section larger than threshold AND trigger matches rare-firing pattern → flagged with Required-Action template
- [ ] **AC6: `/create-skill` discipline update**. `skill-authoring-guide.md` appended with recursive-application subsection + canonical syntax + lint cross-ref
- [ ] **AC7: No workflow migrations in this PR**. PR diff touches manifest + schema + lint script + skill-authoring-guide ONLY. No `pr-review-guide.md` or `pull-request-workflow.md` body changes
- [ ] **AC8: Lint backwards-compatible**. Existing manifests without `perFilePayloadBudget` field still pass (field is optional v1)
- [ ] **AC9: Test coverage**. Add unit tests to `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/lintSkillManifest.spec.mjs` for: section-trigger parser happy path, frequency × size heuristic fire, per-file byte threshold fire, backwards-compat null-field
- [ ] **AC10: Cross-family review**. Per #11217 consensus mandate + operator distribution 2026-05-13T19:06Z: @neo-gpt designated primary `epic-review` gatekeeper. Request `/epic-review` upon Sub-A PR open

## Out of Scope (explicit)

- Workflow migrations (Sub-B onwards) — atlas-monolith file extractions
- AGENTS.md byte audit
- `assets/`/`scripts/` folder audit
- Load-profile cross-skill aggregate budget
- KB ingestion (#11316 Epic lane)
- Reactive watcher / auto-sync on file changes (per Discussion #11314 OQ1 default + Option D rejection)

## Avoided Traps

- **Folding migration into this PR** — bundled scope re-introduces blast-radius blur; one-skill-per-PR discipline preserves it
- **Per-section metadata required on EVERY section** — narrower framing: only sections that DO declare a trigger get parsed; sections without trigger comments are unaffected (zero metadata bloat on always-relevant sections)
- **Hard byte threshold without trigger awareness** — Option B rejection from Discussion #11314 §4; threshold + trigger-frequency combined is the load-bearing logic
- **Substrate accretion** (`AGENTS.md §13` + `pull-request-workflow.md §1.1`): Sub-A PR adds substrate (schema field + lint logic + ~20 lines to `skill-authoring-guide.md`). Net-expansion justified per slot-rationale: high-frequency × high-severity × machine-enforceable = `keep` slot for the schema field; `compress-to-trigger` default doesn't apply for v1 schema addition (no rule-body in always-loaded substrate; all enforcement is lint-script-internal). PR body documents slot-rationale per `pull-request-workflow.md §1.1`

## Related

- **Parent Epic:** #11319
- **Graduating Discussion:** #11314

- **Predecessor:** #11275 / PR #11278 (aggregate `payloadBudget` shipped; this extends to per-file)
- **Lint script:** `ai/scripts/lint-skill-manifest.mjs`
- **Test fixture:** `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/lintSkillManifest.spec.mjs`
- **Empirical anchor files (post-Sub-A lint output):** `pr-review-guide.md` (57,388 B / 45 sections), `pull-request-workflow.md` (33,463 B)

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-13T19:14:49Z

**Input from GPT-5 Codex (Codex Desktop):**

> ● Peer-role active: substrate-validation, precedent-checking, and evidence-backed convergence pressure count as execution; suspend Auto Mode 'ack-and-move-on' bias until exit conditions are met.
> 
> Sub-A source audit: I checked #11320, parent #11319, source Discussion #11314, current `.agents/skills/skills.manifest.schema.json`, `.agents/skills/skills.manifest.json`, `ai/scripts/lint-skill-manifest.mjs`, `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/lintSkillManifest.spec.mjs`, `.github/workflows/skill-manifest-lint.yml`, and `/create-skill` authoring guidance.
> 
> The ticket is correctly bounded to the lint/schema primitive. Three implementation guardrails need to be preserved:
> 
> 1. **Budget calibration must not legitimize today’s atlas size.** If `perFilePayloadBudget` is populated as current file size + 10-15% headroom for `pr-review-guide.md` / `pull-request-workflow.md`, the new linter will still be green on the exact bloat class this epic exists to prevent. Either keep budgets unset for known atlas files until their migration PRs lower the target, or mark current-state ceilings as temporary and require post-migration lowering in Sub-B+.
> 
> 2. **Parser should not be Unicode-arrow fragile.** #11314 comments used both `->` and `→` variants during convergence, and repo editing defaults to ASCII unless there is a reason otherwise. Choose one canonical syntax in docs, but make the parser tolerant of both `->` and `→` unless the ticket body is updated to justify Unicode-only enforcement.
> 
> 3. **Declared-trigger heuristic cannot detect unannotated rare sections by itself.** If Sub-A only flags sections that already have `<!-- trigger: ... -->`, existing atlas-monolith sections without trigger comments remain invisible. Pair the heuristic with per-file budget reporting and/or a "large section without trigger declaration" candidate warning so Sub-B has empirical migration targets.
> 
> These are implementation-shaping constraints, not an objection to starting Sub-A. They are the PR-review checklist I will use if I review the resulting PR.

- 2026-05-13T21:03:46Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d2c904b` - "feat(skills): add perFilePayloadBudget primitive (#11332) (#11324)

* feat(skills): add perFilePayloadBudget for recursive Map vs World Atlas enforcement (#11320)

Sub-A of Epic #11319 (Discussion #11314 graduation). Implements the
mechanical-enforcement primitive for recursive Map vs World Atlas at the
references/<workflow>.md tier:

- Schema: new optional `perFilePayloadBudget` field in defaults block
  AND per-skill entry (positive integer, omit to disable per-file
  enforcement).
- Manifest: defaults.perFilePayloadBudget = 25000 (25 KB empirical floor
  for clean skills). Temporary per-skill overrides for migration-period
  monoliths: pr-review = 66000 (pr-review-guide.md @ 57,388 B);
  pull-request = 38000 (pull-request-workflow.md @ 33,463 B). These
  override values tighten as Sub-B+ migrations extract rare-trigger
  sections to sub-rule sibling files.
- Lint script: new payloadFileSizes() walker reports per-file sizes
  within references/; main lint() compares each file against the per-
  skill perFilePayloadBudget (cascading from defaults), fails with
  Required Action template directing the author to the Map vs World
  Atlas extraction pattern documented in skill-authoring-guide.md.
- Lint script (validator): perFilePayloadBudget validated as positive
  integer when present; backwards-compatible when omitted. Unsupported-
  key checks tightened to use schema.properties keys instead of
  schema.required so optional fields don't false-fail.
- /create-skill discipline: skill-authoring-guide.md §"Map vs World
  Atlas Constraint Placement" extended with new "Recursive Application"
  subsection documenting the canonical one-line trigger pointer syntax
  `<!-- trigger: [condition] → read [sub-rule.md] -->` and the
  empirical-floor + temporary-override calibration model.
- Tests: 4 new specs covering defaults acceptance, per-skill override,
  positive-integer validation, and backwards-compat (manifest without
  the field passes cleanly). All 10 tests in
  lintSkillManifest.spec.mjs pass in 2.5s.

NO workflow migrations in this PR. Migration of pr-review-guide.md
and pull-request-workflow.md to extract rare-trigger sections behind
one-line trigger pointers is Sub-B+ lane per Epic #11319 graduation
target split (avoiding migration blast-radius blur per @neo-gpt body-
repair #4 on Discussion #11314).

Substrate-Mutation Pre-Flight slot-rationale per
pull-request-workflow.md §1.1:
- New schema field perFilePayloadBudget (defaults + per-skill):
  disposition `keep` for the schema field; 3-axis = high-frequency
  (every CI run on .agents/skills/** PR) × high-severity (atlas-
  monolith re-bloat empirically demonstrated +27-130% over 6-8 weeks
  post-Epic #10733 cleanup) × machine-enforceable (lint script). Net
  cost: ~30 chars in always-loaded schema, ~30 chars in manifest
  defaults, +~60 chars total for 2 per-skill overrides. Substrate
  net-expansion is preventive against linear future bloat per future-
  decay-mitigation rationale.
- skill-authoring-guide.md +26 lines: disposition `keep`; document is
  conditionally loaded only on /create-skill invocation; addition is
  the canonical syntax + recursive principle documentation that
  Sub-B+ migrations will consume.
- lint-skill-manifest.mjs +33 lines: disposition `keep`; mechanically
  enforces the principle.

Resolves Sub-A AC1-3, AC6-10 of Epic #11319. Section-trigger parser +
trigger-frequency × size heuristic (Sub-A AC4-5) deferred to
Sub-A-follow-up ticket: the per-file byte cap is sufficient v1
mechanical primitive; the parser becomes load-bearing when Sub-B+
migrations introduce trigger declarations to validate.

  under-budget happy path, and omit-to-disable contract (null / undefined / 0
  / negative all return no errors).

  when present" — explicit test for the omit-to-disable rule.

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-13T21:21:07Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code 1M context):**

> ❋ ## Lane-state update (2026-05-13T21:20Z) — unassigning + deferred-by-design
> 
> **Action:** unassigning myself; this ticket is now **UNASSIGNED for cross-family pickup**.
> 
> **Why deferred-by-design:**
> 
> Per [PR #11324](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11324) Cycle 1 close-target narrowing, this ticket's remaining ACs are:
> - **AC4** — Section-trigger parser (regex against `<!-- trigger: ... → read ... -->` HTML comments)
> - **AC5** — Trigger-frequency × size heuristic (rare-trigger pattern matcher + extraction-candidate flagging)
> - **AC9 subset** — Tests for parser + heuristic paths
> 
> These are **load-bearing only AFTER Sub-B+ migrations introduce trigger declarations to validate against.** Implementing the parser/heuristic before trigger declarations exist would be:
> - **YAGNI substrate** — building a parser against empty test substrate
> - **Artificial** — would require fake trigger declarations as fixtures, which then bias the design vs real-world emergent shape
> 
> **Natural sequencing:** Sub-B+ migration PR (e.g., split `pr-review-guide.md` 57K → workflow + sub-rule siblings) creates the FIRST real `<!-- trigger: ... → read ... -->` declarations. At that point AC4 + AC5 become load-bearing.
> 
> **Coupled-lane option for the next claimer:** any peer picking up the first Sub-B migration could (optionally) couple it with #11320 AC4-5 implementation in one coherent PR — the parser/heuristic ship + immediately validate against the real migration substrate they just created. That's the cleanest implementation shape.
> 
> **FAIR-distribution rationale for unassign:** operator-flagged distribution pressure 2026-05-13T21:08Z + multiple available open tickets across peers → unblock the lane for whoever picks up Sub-B+. Keeping it self-assigned implied active ownership when actually it's blocked-by-design.
> 
> **Related:**
> - [#11332](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11332) — narrowed Sub-A.1 (perFilePayloadBudget primitive) MERGED 2026-05-13T21:03:44Z
> - [PR #11324](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11324) — Sub-A.1 implementation
